content:
Input: On receipt of the Stop signal, the software turns OFF the Heater, turns OFF the Cooking Lamp, and enters a wait state.
Expected Output:
Objects of Interest:
- Stop button: Stop signal
- Heater: Heater On/Off command
- Cooking lamp: Cooking lamp On/Off command

Input: On receipt of a 30-second signal the software receives the 30-second signal, waits for and receives the elapsed time signal, retrieves the current cooking mode from the RAM, gets a new target temperature for this cooking mode by obtaining it from the relationship stored in the ROM and stores this new target temperature into the RAM.
Expected Output:
Objects of Interest:
- Timer: 30-second signal, Current elapsed time signal
- Current settings: Current cooking mode, Current target temperature
- Target settings: New target temperature

Input: On receipt of the Start signal, the software sends Turn ON signals to both the Cooking Lamp and the Heater.
Expected Output:
Objects of Interest:
- Start Button: start_signal
- Cooking Lamp: on_off_command
- Heater: on_off_command

Input: When a Registrar wishes to enter data about a new Professor, he selects the sub-option 'Add Professor' and enters Professor details including ID, name, address, social security number, Department, qualifications and contact details.
Expected Output:
Objects of Interest:
- Professor: professor_id, name, address, social_security, department, qualifications, contact_details
- Errors: error_text, validation_message

Input: On receipt of a 5-second signal the software receives the 5-second signal, retrieves the current target temperature from the RAM, retrieves the actual sensor temperature from the Temperature Sensor, sends a Turn ON or OFF command to the Heater.
Expected Output:
Functional Process: Check cooker temperature
Triggering Event: 5-second signal
Functional User: Timer
Triggering Entry: 5-second signal received

Input: On receipt of a 30-second signal the software receives the 30-second signal, waits for and receives the elapsed time signal, retrieves the current cooking mode from the RAM, gets a new target temperature for this cooking mode by obtaining it from the relationship stored in the ROM and stores this new target temperature into the RAM.
Expected Output:
Functional Process: Update Target temperature
Triggering Event: 30-second signal
Functional User: Timer
Triggering Entry: 30-second signal received

Input: On receipt of the Start signal, the software sends Turn ON signals to both the Cooking Lamp and the Heater.
Expected Output:
Functional Process: Start Cooking
Triggering Event: Start button pressed
Functional User: Start button
Triggering Entry: Start button pressed

Input: When a Registrar wishes to enter data about a new Professor, he selects the sub-option 'Add Professor' and enters Professor details including ID, name, address, social security number, Department, qualifications and contact details.
Expected Output:
Functional Process: Add a Professor
Triggering Event: A Registrar needs to add a Professor's details
Functional User: Registrar
Triggering Entry: A Registrar needs to add a Professor's details

Input: On receipt of a 5-second signal the software receives the 5-second signal, retrieves the current target temperature from the RAM, retrieves the actual sensor temperature from the Temperature Sensor, sends a Turn ON or OFF command to the Heater.
Expected Output:
Functional Users:
- Timer
- Temperature Sensor
- Heater

Input: On receipt of a 30-second signal the software receives the 30-second signal, waits for and receives the elapsed time signal, retrieves the current cooking mode from the RAM, gets a new target temperature for this cooking mode by obtaining it from the relationship stored in the ROM and stores this new target temperature into the RAM.
Expected Output:
Functional Users:
- Timer

Input: On receipt of the Start signal, the software sends Turn ON signals to both the Cooking Lamp and the Heater.
Expected Output:
Functional Users:
- Start button
- Cooking Lamp
- Heater

Input: When a Registrar wishes to enter data about a new Professor, he selects the sub-option 'Add Professor' and enters Professor details including ID, name, address, social security number, Department, qualifications and contact details.
Expected Output:
Functional Users:
- Registrar

Input: On receipt of a 30-second signal the software receives the 30-second signal, waits for and receives the elapsed time signal, retrieves the current cooking mode from the RAM, gets a new target temperature for this cooking mode by obtaining it from the relationship stored in the ROM and stores this new target temperature into the RAM.
Expected Output:
Sub-processes:
- Sub-process: Receive 30-second signal
  Functional User: Timer
  Moved Data Group: 30-second signal
  Object of Interest: Timer
  Source: Timer
  Destination: system
  Type: Entry
- Sub-process: Receive Elapsed Time
  Functional User: Timer
  Moved Data Group: Current elapsed time signal
  Object of Interest: Timer
  Source: Timer
  Destination: system
  Type: Entry
- Sub-process: Read Cooking Mode
  Functional User: -
  Moved Data Group: Current cooking mode
  Object of Interest: Current settings
  Source: Current settings (RAM)
  Destination: system
  Type: Read
- Sub-process: Read Target Temperature
  Functional User: -
  Moved Data Group: New target temperature
  Object of Interest: Target settings
  Source: Target settings (ROM)
  Destination: system
  Type: Read
- Sub-process: Write Target Temperature
  Functional User: -
  Moved Data Group: Current target temperature
  Object of Interest: Current settings
  Source: system
  Destination: Current settings (RAM)
  Type: Write

Input: On receipt of a 5-second signal the software receives the 5-second signal, retrieves the current target temperature from the RAM, retrieves the actual sensor temperature from the Temperature Sensor, sends a Turn ON or OFF command to the Heater.
Expected Output:
Sub-processes:
- Sub-process: Receive 5-second signal
  Functional User: Timer
  Moved Data Group: 5-second signal
  Object of Interest: Timer
  Source: Timer
  Destination: system
  Type: Entry
- Sub-process: Read Target Temperature
  Functional User: -
  Moved Data Group: Current target temperature
  Object of Interest: Current settings
  Source: Current settings (RAM)
  Destination: system
  Type: Read
- Sub-process: Receive Sensor Temperature
  Functional User: Temperature Sensor
  Moved Data Group: Actual sensor temperature
  Object of Interest: Temperature sensor
  Source: Temperature Sensor
  Destination: system
  Type: Entry
- Sub-process: Send Heater Command
  Functional User: Heater
  Moved Data Group: Heater On/Off command
  Object of Interest: Heater
  Source: system
  Destination: Heater
  Type: Exit

Input: On receipt of the Start signal, the software sends Turn ON signals to both the Cooking Lamp and the Heater.
Expected Output:
Sub-processes:
- Sub-process: Receive Start Signal
  Functional User: Start button
  Moved Data Group: Start signal
  Object of Interest: Start Button
  Source: Start button
  Destination: system
  Type: Entry
- Sub-process: Send Heater Command
  Functional User: Heater
  Moved Data Group: Heater On/Off command
  Object of Interest: Heater
  Source: system
  Destination: Heater
  Type: Exit
- Sub-process: Send Lamp Command
  Functional User: Cooking Lamp
  Moved Data Group: Cooking lamp On/Off command
  Object of Interest: Cooking Lamp
  Source: system
  Destination: Cooking Lamp
  Type: Exit

Input: When a Registrar wishes to enter data about a new Professor, he selects the sub-option 'Add Professor' and enters Professor details including ID, name, address, social security number, Department, qualifications and contact details.
Expected Output:
Sub-processes:
- Sub-process: Enter Professor
  Functional User: Registrar
  Moved Data Group: Professor details
  Object of Interest: Professor
  Source: Registrar
  Destination: system
  Type: Entry
- Sub-process: Read Professor
  Functional User: N/A
  Moved Data Group: Professor details
  Object of Interest: Professor
  Source: storage
  Destination: system
  Type: Read
- Sub-process: Create Professor
  Functional User: N/A
  Moved Data Group: Professor details
  Object of Interest: Professor
  Source: system
  Destination: storage
  Type: Write
- Sub-process: Display Error Message
  Functional User: Registrar
  Moved Data Group: Error Messages
  Object of Interest: Errors
  Source: system
  Destination: Registrar
  Type: Exit

Input: When a Professor creates Course Offering commitments: after viewing the list of eligible Course Offerings, the Professor selects the Offerings. The system sends the selected Course Offering IDs to the Course Catalog, receives the count of conflicting Course Offerings and their IDs, then displays either the conflicts or a confirmation message.

Expected Output:
Sub-processes:
- Sub-process: Select Course Offerings
  Functional User: Professor
  Moved Data Group: Professor’s Course Offering selections
  Object of Interest: Course Offering
  Source: Professor
  Destination: System
  Type: Entry

- Sub-process: Send Course Offerings
  Functional User: Course Catalog
  Moved Data Group: Professor’s Course Offering selections
  Object of Interest: Course Offering
  Source: System
  Destination: Course Catalog
  Type: Exit

- Sub-process: Receive Conflict Count
  Functional User: Course Catalog
  Moved Data Group: Count of conflicting courses
  Object of Interest: Course Offering
  Source: Course Catalog
  Destination: System
  Type: Entry

- Sub-process: Receive Conflicting Offerings
  Functional User: Course Catalog
  Moved Data Group: Conflicting Course Offering selections
  Object of Interest: Course Offering
  Source: Course Catalog
  Destination: System
  Type: Entry

- Sub-process: Display Conflicts
  Functional User: Professor
  Moved Data Group: Conflicting Course Offering selections
  Object of Interest: Course Offering
  Source: System
  Destination: Professor
  Type: Exit

- Sub-process: Display Confirmation
  Functional User: Professor
  Moved Data Group: Confirmation Message
  Object of Interest: Confirmation
  Source: System
  Destination: Professor
  Type: Exit
